.ddx/executions/20260506T095839-9b2751c7/manifest.json:11:    "title": "Ship human-writing-support skill in the default DDx plugin",
.ddx/executions/20260506T095839-9b2751c7/manifest.json:12:    "description": "CONTEXT
DDx should guide agents while they write or edit prose, separate from deterministic post-write checks. This bead ships a default-plugin skill named human-writing-support that adapts the stronger advisory guidance from human-writing-style approaches to DDx: preserve voice, prefer specific detail, avoid generic AI-like tropes, and do not trade away technical precision.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- library/.agents/skills/ddx/human-writing-support/SKILL.md or the default-plugin skill path established by FEAT-011/TD-036
- skills/ddx/human-writing-support/SKILL.md and .agents/.claude materialized copies only if this repo maintains generated checked-in mirrors
- skill validation/eval fixtures under the existing skills validation surface

REQUIRED SKILL BEHAVIOR
- Trigger for writing, rewriting, editing, or reviewing DDx prose, specs, ADRs, beads, docs, website copy, and public prose.
- Explicitly says it is not an AI detector or detector-bypass tool.
- Preserves user voice and technical precision.
- Applies different expectations for technical, planning, and public prose.
- Encourages concrete/checkable detail over generic importance language.
- Directs agents to run deterministic prose checks when available.

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- CLI checker implementation.
- Rule pack implementation.
- Bead review integration.",

2. `rg -n "not an AI detector|detector bypass|specific|voice|technical|planning|public|ddx doc prose"` against the skill file returns matches for required guidance.
3. Existing skill validation command passes, or if this repo uses EXEC-011, the focused validation for shipped skills passes.
4. The skill does not require agents to remove legitimate technical lists, headings, or DDx terminology.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.",

Steps:
1. Add Makefile target copy-skills in cli/Makefile that rsyncs skills/ddx/ into cli/internal/skills/ddx/ before every build. Example:
     copy-skills:

2. Wire copy-skills as a prerequisite of make build, make test, make dev (and any other targets that invoke go build or go test).
3. Update cli/internal/skills/embed.go to replace the current multi-dir embed line with: //go:embed all:ddx
4. Delete cli/internal/skills/ddx-{bead,agent,doctor,install,status,review,run}/ ŌĆö the new embed picks up the copied ddx/ tree instead. (Deletion of top-level /skills/ddx-* is Phase 2; this bead only touches cli/internal/skills/.)
5. Commit cli/internal/skills/ddx/ as a tracked copy so contributors who run go build directly (without make) still get a valid binary. Add a pre-commit hook (lefthook.yml) that re-runs make copy-skills if skills/ddx/ is dirty and cli/internal/skills/ddx/ is stale; this keeps the copy in sync without manual intervention.

In-scope files:
- cli/Makefile
- cli/internal/skills/embed.go
- cli/internal/skills/ddx/ (committed copy ŌĆö gets populated by copy-skills)
- lefthook.yml (add pre-commit hook)

- ddx init / ddx update changes ŌĆö separate beads
- Old /skills/ddx-* (top-level) deletion ŌĆö Phase 2

Document-Driven Development eXperience

DDx is a toolkit for AI-assisted development that helps you:

ŌĆó Share templates, prompts, and patterns across projects
ŌĆó Maintain consistent development practices
ŌĆó Integrate AI tooling seamlessly
ŌĆó Contribute improvements back to the community

Get started:
  ddx init          Initialize DDx in your project
  ddx list          See available resources
  ddx doctor        Check installation and diagnose issues

More information:
  Documentation: https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx
  Issues & Support: https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/issues

Usage:
  ddx [command]

Available Commands:
  agent       Invoke AI agents with harness dispatch, quorum, and session logging
  bead        Manage beads (portable work items)
  checkpoint  Create, list, or restore named git checkpoints
  completion  Generate completion script
  config      Configure DDx settings
  doc         Document dependency graph and staleness tracking
  doctor      Check installation health and diagnose issues
  exec        Validate and run DDx execution definitions
  help        Help about any command
  init        Initialize DDx in current project
  install     Install a package or resource
  installed   List installed packages
  jq          Process JSON with jq expressions
  list        List available DDx resources
  log         Show DDX asset history
  metric      Validate and run metric definitions
  metrics     Show process metrics derived from beads and agent sessions
  outdated    List installed packages with available updates
  persona     Manage AI personas for consistent interactions
  prompts     Manage AI prompts
  search      Search available packages
  server      Start the DDx HTTP and MCP server
  skills      Validate bundled skill packages
  status      Show version and status information
  uninstall   Remove an installed package
  update      Update DDx CLI or installed plugins
  upgrade     Upgrade DDx to the latest version
  verify      Verify installed package integrity
  version     Show version information
  work        Work the bead execution queue

Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
  -h, --help                       help for ddx
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

Plan file: /home/erik/.claude/plans/let-s-review-the-skills-synchronous-kite.md (reference only ŌĆö do not require reading it; beads inline the context).

Governing spec: docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md (rewritten to reflect this design).

Motivation: Today ddx init ships 2 of 7 embedded skills, skill trees have drifted between /skills/ and cli/internal/skills/, HELIX opinions leak into ddx-bead, and users must guess between /ddx-run vs /ddx-work vs /ddx-bead. The single-skill design routes every natural-language intent ("do work", "drain the queue", "review this") into the same skill, then into the right reference file.

This epic contains 10 child beads covering: (a) create SKILL.md + 5 reference/*.md files; (b) wire embed.go + Makefile copy-skills target; (c) update ddx init for single skill + stale-dir cleanup + AGENTS.md merge markers; (d) update ddx update refresh path; (e) ship eval suite (routing.jsonl + eval-skill.sh) for CI enforcement of cross-harness routing behavior.","id":"ddx-f586b4fd","issue_type":"epic","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:skills","area:agent-experience"],"priority":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-011","status":"closed","title":"Epic: Consolidate DDx skills into a single portable `ddx` skill","updated_at":"2026-04-17T22:32:57.448488088Z"}
.ddx/executions/20260506T091604-645ceb67/manifest.json:11:    "title": "Ship human-writing-support skill in the default DDx plugin",
.ddx/executions/20260506T091604-645ceb67/manifest.json:12:    "description": "CONTEXT
DDx should guide agents while they write or edit prose, separate from deterministic post-write checks. This bead ships a default-plugin skill named human-writing-support that adapts the stronger advisory guidance from human-writing-style approaches to DDx: preserve voice, prefer specific detail, avoid generic AI-like tropes, and do not trade away technical precision.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- library/.agents/skills/ddx/human-writing-support/SKILL.md or the default-plugin skill path established by FEAT-011/TD-036
- skills/ddx/human-writing-support/SKILL.md and .agents/.claude materialized copies only if this repo maintains generated checked-in mirrors
- skill validation/eval fixtures under the existing skills validation surface

REQUIRED SKILL BEHAVIOR
- Trigger for writing, rewriting, editing, or reviewing DDx prose, specs, ADRs, beads, docs, website copy, and public prose.
- Explicitly says it is not an AI detector or detector-bypass tool.
- Preserves user voice and technical precision.
- Applies different expectations for technical, planning, and public prose.
- Encourages concrete/checkable detail over generic importance language.
- Directs agents to run deterministic prose checks when available.

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- CLI checker implementation.
- Rule pack implementation.
- Bead review integration.",
.ddx/executions/20260506T091604-645ceb67/manifest.json:13:    "acceptance": "1. The default DDx plugin includes a human-writing-support skill with valid agentskills.io frontmatter and a trigger description under the project convention limit.
2. `rg -n "not an AI detector|detector bypass|specific|voice|technical|planning|public|ddx doc prose"` against the skill file returns matches for required guidance.
3. Existing skill validation command passes, or if this repo uses EXEC-011, the focused validation for shipped skills passes.
4. The skill does not require agents to remove legitimate technical lists, headings, or DDx terminology.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.",

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01RRrti2GrbEf4wPAzgSbqPQ","type":"tool_result","content":"#!/usr/bin/env bash\n# Verifies every `path:line` citation in SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md\n# points to a file that exists and a line that exists. When invoked with\n# --strict, also verifies the line contains a Go symbol hint when the path\n# looks like Go source.\n#\n# Cited as the CI step that prevents SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md from\n# rotting silently as routing.go / discovery.go / types.go evolve.\n\nset -euo pipefail\n\nDOC_PATH=\"${1:-docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md}\"\n\nif [ ! -f \"$DOC_PATH\" ]; then\n    echo \"citations: $DOC_PATH not found\" >&2\n    exit 1\nfi\n\n# Match citations of the form `path/to/file.ext:NN` inside backticks.\n# We only validate citations whose path looks like a file (contains a slash\n# and ends with a known extension).\ncitations=$(grep -oE '`[A-Za-z0-9_./-]+\\.(go|ts|tsx|js|yaml|yml|md|sh):[0-9]+`' \"$DOC_PATH\" | tr -d '`' | sort -u)\n\nif [ -z \"$citations\" ]; then\n    echo \"citations: no citations found in $DOC_PATH ŌĆö fail-closed\" >&2\n    exit 1\nfi\n\nfailures=0\ntotal=0\n\nwhile IFS= read -r cite; do\n    [ -z \"$cite\" ] && continue\n    total=$((total + 1))\n    path=\"${cite%:*}\"\n    line=\"${cite##*:}\"\n    if [ ! -f \"$path\" ]; then\n        echo \"citations: MISSING FILE: $cite\" >&2\n        failures=$((failures + 1))\n        continue\n    fi\n    # Line count check; awk 'END{print NR}' counts lines portably.\n    nlines=$(awk 'END{print NR}' \"$path\")\n    if [ \"$line\" -gt \"$nlines\" ]; then\n        echo \"citations: LINE OUT OF RANGE: $cite (file has $nlines lines)\" >&2\n        failures=$((failures + 1))\n        continue\n    fi\n    # Sanity check: the line must not be blank, since every real citation\n    # points at a struct, func, or statement.\n    content=$(awk -v n=\"$line\" 'NR==n' \"$path\")\n    if [ -z \"$(echo \"$content\" | tr -d '[:space:]')\" ]; then\n        echo \"citations: BLANK LINE: $cite\" >&2\n        failures=$((failures + 1))\n        continue\n    fi\ndone <<< \"$citations\"\n\nif [ \"$failures\" -gt 0 ]; then\n    echo \"citations: $failures/$total failed for $DOC_PATH\" >&2\n    exit 1\nfi\n\necho \"citations: $total/$total ok for $DOC_PATH\"","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"d932d409-7007-4d29-af52-a78f704e35c0","uuid":"465c02eb-3d0f-4e0e-9149-057b595f6ab5","timestamp":"2026-04-18T06:48:07.386Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"#!/usr/bin/env bash\n# Verifies every `path:line` citation in SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md\n# points to a file that exists and a line that exists. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01FbNtbFVUbDesKpUsBMMxbR","type":"tool_result","content":"470\t\n471\t// scoreCandidate returns a score for a viable candidate given the profile.\n472\t// Higher is better.\n473\t//\n474\t// Routing priority policy:\n475\t//   - cheap/standard: local (free, no quota) > subscription-within-quota > pay-per-token\n476\t//   - smart: cloud capability wins; local models are typically quantized and lower-quality\n477\tfunc scoreCandidate(profile string, plan CandidatePlan) float64 {\n478\t\tbase := 100.0\n479\t\n480\t\tcr := costClassRank[plan.CostClass] // 0=local,1=cheap,2=medium,3=expensive\n481\t\n482\t\t// withinQuota is true when a subscription harness has headroom (cost already sunk).\n483\t\twithinQuota := plan.IsSubscription && (plan.State.QuotaOK || plan.State.QuotaState == \"ok\")\n484\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01BpB5ybRXLsC8V3pvMEmJe9","session_id":"d932d409-7007-4d29-af52-a78f704e35c0","uuid":"93141aca-fb97-4955-80e0-dc1986fc1d6f","timestamp":"2026-04-18T06:48:53.965Z"}

What is done
- Verified the bead's stated hard-block precondition still holds: V9 cannot run for
  exactly the same infrastructure reasons that closed V6 (ddx-ea3a7e23) as no_changes.
- Confirmed by inspection of this worktree:
  1. .agents/skills/nano-banana-pro-openrouter/ does not exist. Only ddx and helix-*
     skills are present under .agents/skills/. The image-generation skill referenced
     by the plan's generator pipeline is still missing.
  2. `ddx exec list --json` returns `[]`. There is no nano-banana-pro-openrouter
     execution definition registered, and nothing lists any tool-graphic artifact
     under produces_artifact.
  3. `ddx artifact regenerate tools-composite` fails with
     "artifact \"tools-composite\" not found in document graph" ŌĆö the artifact is
     not registered. selectArtifactGenerator (cli/cmd/artifact.go:99) would also
     error with "no generator definition or generated_by provenance".
  4. The V8 sibling bead (ddx-1848bbaa "author 4 tool-graphic prompts") is closed,
     but docs/helix/00-discover/visuals/ contains only the six principle prompts ŌĆö
     no tool-graphic prompt files exist. There is no prompt input for V9 to consume
     even if generation infrastructure were available.

What is blocking
1. Missing image-generation skill (nano-banana-pro-openrouter) under
   .agents/skills/. Symlinks under .crush/skills/ and .pi/skills/ point at a
   directory that doesn't exist here.
2. No registered execution definition (`ddx exec list --json` is empty), so
   `ddx artifact regenerate` has no generator to dispatch to.
3. No registered tool-graphic artifacts in the document graph. Without sidecars
   declaring id + media_type + depends_on, regenerate can't resolve them.
4. No tool-graphic prompt sources in docs/helix/00-discover/visuals/, despite
   V8 (ddx-1848bbaa) being closed. V9's contract assumes those prompts exist.
5. The plan explicitly forbids manual prompt-file ŌåÆ image fallbacks
   (plan-2026-04-29-visual-suite.md:255). V9 must run through the artifact
   pipeline or not run at all.

What a follow-up attempt would need (in order)
1. Re-open or supersede V8 (ddx-1848bbaa) ŌĆö the four tool-graphic prompt files
   it was supposed to author are not in the worktree, so V9's input contract
   is also unmet. Verify the closure was substantive, not a no_changes.
2. Predecessor bead: install / vendor nano-banana-pro-openrouter into
   .agents/skills/ so the broken symlinks resolve.
3. Predecessor bead: author `ddx exec define` for the nano-banana-pro-openrouter
   generator with produces_artifact entries for each tool graphic plus
   tools-composite.
4. Predecessor bead: register the four tool-graphic artifacts and tools-composite
   in the document graph (sidecars with id, media_type image/png, depends_on
   the V8 prompts and DESIGN.md tokens).
5. Once 1ŌĆō4 hold, V9 collapses to: budget-tracked sequential
   `ddx artifact regenerate <each-tool-graphic>` then
   `ddx artifact regenerate tools-composite`, evaluating each render against
   the bead's acceptance criteria (placement, contrast, mobile crop, alt text,
   file size, metaphor parses without copy, brand-fit / no-kitsch),
   halting at $1.50/image or $10 total.

Recommendation
File the four predecessor beads above, block ddx-297e7b4b on them, and re-queue.
Do not escalate to a stronger model ŌĆö the constraint is missing infrastructure
and missing inputs, not model capability. This mirrors the V6 (ddx-ea3a7e23)
disposition exactly; resolving the infrastructure unblocks both the principles
and tools lenses simultaneously.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:5:{"acceptance":"- [ ] `RunOptions` accepts a caller ctx, or `Runner.Run`/`Runner.RunAgent` accept ctx explicitly
- [ ] The internal cancel is derived from the caller ctx (no `context.Background()` seeds inside Run/RunAgent)
- [ ] `agent.ExecuteBead` takes ctx and passes it down
- [ ] `server.WorkerManager.Stop(id)` cancels the running agent provider call within 2s in a unit test (fake provider blocks on ctx)
- [ ] Regression test: runner with a canceled ctx returns promptly (<500ms) regardless of mocked provider latency","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T04:51:41Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"9b044bcebc981533d2d48d4ffd79a91743dee884","created_at":"2026-04-18T04:41:24.464808955Z","description":"## Root cause RC1 from ddx-0a651925

`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:239` (Runner.Run) and `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:163` (Runner.RunAgent) both call `context.WithCancel(context.Background())`, discarding any context from the caller. `RunOptions` exposes no ctx field. `agent.ExecuteBead` likewise takes no ctx, so `server.WorkerManager.runWorker`'s ctx never reaches the agent call.

As a result, `WorkerManager.Stop(id)` (server/workers.go:648) cancels only the worker goroutine; the embedded `agentlib.Run` call (and any subprocess it launched) keeps running until SIGKILL. This is the structural defect behind 33ŌĆō142h hung workers.

## Scope

1. Add `Context context.Context` to `RunOptions` (or change signatures to `Run(ctx, opts)`). Default to `context.Background()` when nil.
2. Derive the internal ctx from the caller's rather than `context.Background()`.
3. Thread ctx through `agent.ExecuteBead` ŌåÆ `runner.Run` ŌåÆ `agentlib.Run` ŌåÆ provider HTTP.
4. Update `server.WorkerManager.runWorker`'s `singleTierAttempt` closure to pass `ctx` into `agent.ExecuteBead`.

## Non-goals

- New wall-clock timeout logic (covered by sibling RC2 bead)
- Operator-facing stop command (covered by ddx-b808df39)

Regression anchor for ddx-0a651925 RC1: runner.Run and\n+// Runner.RunAgent previously discarded the caller's ctx by constructing\n+// their own context.WithCancel(context.Background()), so WorkerManager.Stop\n+// could not cancel the in-flight agent call.\n+func TestRunAgentCancelledContextReturnsPromptly(t *testing.T) {\n+\tprovider := &sleepProvider{\n+\t\tdelay: 30 * time.Second, // far larger than the 500ms AC deadline\n+\t\tresponse: agentlib.Response{\n+\t\t\tContent: \"never-returned\",\n+\t\t\tModel:   \"test-model\",\n+\t\t},\n+\t}\n+\n+\tr := NewRunner(Config{SessionLogDir: t.TempDir()})\n+\tr.LookPath = mockLookPath\n+\tr.AgentProvider = provider\n+\n+\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n+\tcancel() // pre-cancel so the call returns on entry\n+\n+\tstart := time.Now()\n+\tresult, err := r.RunAgent(RunOptions{\n+\t\tHarness: \"agent\",\n+\t\tPrompt:  \"test\",\n+\t\tContext: ctx,\n+\t})\n+\telapsed := time.Since(start)\n+\n+\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n+\trequire.NotNil(t, result)\n+\tassert.Equal(t, 1, result.ExitCode, \"canceled run should report non-zero exit\")\n+\tassert.NotEmpty(t, result.Error, \"canceled run should carry error text\")\n+\tassert.Less(t, elapsed, 500*time.Millisecond,\n+\t\t\"canceled ctx must abort the provider call promptly; took %v\", elapsed)\n+}\n+\n+// TestRunAgentContextCancelledMidRunReturnsPromptly cancels the caller's\n+// context while the provider is blocked, then asserts that the runner\n+// unwinds within 500ms of cancellation. This exercises the propagation\n+// path (caller ctx ŌåÆ internal WithCancel(parent) ŌåÆ agentlib.Run ŌåÆ provider\n+// Chat) rather than the short-circuit pre-canceled case.\n+func TestRunAgentContextCancelledMidRunReturnsPromptly(t *testing.T) {\n+\tprovider := &sleepProvider{\n+\t\tdelay: 30 * time.Second,\n+\t\tresponse: agentlib.Response{\n+\t\t\tContent: \"never-returned\",\n+\t\t\tModel:   \"test-model\",\n+\t\t},\n+\t}\n+\n+\tr := NewRunner(Config{SessionLogDir: t.TempDir()})\n+\tr.LookPath = mockLookPath\n+\tr.AgentProvider = provider\n+\n+\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n+\t// Cancel after a brief warmup so we measure the cancelŌåÆreturn latency.\n+\tgo func() {\n+\t\ttime.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)\n+\t\tcancel()\n+\t}()\n+\n+\tstart := time.Now()\n+\tresult, err := r.RunAgent(RunOptions{\n+\t\tHarness: \"agent\",\n+\t\tPrompt:  \"test\",\n+\t\tContext: ctx,\n+\t})\n+\telapsed := time.Since(start)\n+\n+\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n+\trequire.NotNil(t, result)\n+\tassert.Equal(t, 1, result.ExitCode)\n+\tassert.Less(t, elapsed, 500*time.Millisecond,\n+\t\t\"caller cancel must propagate within 500ms; took %v\", elapsed)\n+}\n+\n+// TestRunCancelledContextReturnsPromptlySubprocess verifies the same\n+// cancellation contract for the subprocess dispatch path through Runner.Run.\n+// The OSExecutor honours ctx by sending SIGKILL on ctx.Done, so a canceled\n+// ctx must not leave the runner blocked on the child exit.\n+func TestRunCancelledContextReturnsPromptlySubprocess(t *testing.T) {\n+\t// Use the mockExecutor (in-process fake) with a blocking delay. A\n+\t// canceled context should short-circuit execution before any exec\n+\t// happens, rather than waiting for the mock.\n+\texec := &blockingMockExecutor{block: 30 * time.Second}\n+\tr := newTestRunner(&mockExecutor{output: \"ok\"})\n+\tr.Executor = exec\n+\n+\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n+\tcancel()\n+\n+\tstart := time.Now()\n+\tresult, err := r.Run(RunOptions{\n+\t\tHarness: \"codex\",\n+\t\tPrompt:  \"hello\",\n+\t\tContext: ctx,\n+\t})\n+\telapsed := time.Since(start)\n+\n+\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n+\trequire.NotNil(t, result)\n+\tassert.Less(t, elapsed, 500*time.Millisecond,\n+\t\t\"canceled ctx must abort subprocess dispatch promptly; took %v\", elapsed)\n+}\n+\n+// blockingMockExecutor is an Executor whose ExecuteInDir respects ctx: it\n+// either blocks for `block` or returns ctx.Err() when ctx is canceled.\n+type blockingMockExecutor struct {\n+\tblock time.Duration\n+}\n+\n+func (e *blockingMockExecutor) Execute(ctx context.Context, binary string, args []string, stdin string) (*ExecResult, error) {\n+\treturn e.ExecuteInDir(ctx, binary, args, stdin, \"\")\n+}\n+\n+func (e *blockingMockExecutor) ExecuteInDir(ctx context.Context, _ string, _ []string, _ string, _ string) (*ExecResult, error) {\n+\tselect {\n+\tcase <-time.After(e.block):\n+\t\treturn &ExecResult{Stdout: \"\", ExitCode: 0}, nil\n+\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n+\t\treturn &ExecResult{ExitCode: -1}, ctx.Err()\n+\t}\n+}\ndiff --git a/cli/internal/server/workers_stop_propagation_test.go b/cli/internal/server/workers_stop_propagation_test.go\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 00000000..4b7ce402\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/cli/internal/server/workers_stop_propagation_test.go\n@@ -0,0 +1,86 @@\n+package server\n+\n+import (\n+\t\"context\"\n+\t\"sync/atomic\"\n+\t\"testing\"\n+\t\"time\"\n+\n+\t\"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent\"\n+\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert\"\n+\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n+)\n+\n+// TestWorkerManagerStopCancelsAgentExecution is the regression test for\n+// ddx-0a651925 RC1: WorkerManager.Stop(id) must cancel the in-flight\n+// executor (and transitively the agent provider call) via ctx propagation,\n+// not merely close the poll loop. With ctx threaded into RunOptions.Context, Stop's\n+// cancellation propagates into the executor within seconds.\n+//\n+// The fake executor here blocks on ctx (exactly like a real provider's\n+// 30 * time.Second,\n+\t})\n+\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n+\n+\t// Wait until the executor has actually started running so we know the\n+\t// cancellation we exercise is against an in-flight call.\n+\trequire.Eventually(t, executorStarted.Load, 3*time.Second, 20*time.Millisecond,\n+\t\t\"executor never entered the blocking Chat call\")\n+\n+\tstopStart := time.Now()\n+\trequire.NoError(t, m.Stop(record.ID))\n+\n+\tselect {\n+\tcase <-executorDone:\n+\tcase <-time.After(2 * time.Second):\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"executor was not canceled within 2s of WorkerManager.Stop\")\n+\t}\n+\telapsed := time.Since(stopStart)\n+\tassert.Less(t, elapsed, 2*time.Second,\n+\t\t\"Stop should cancel the running agent call within 2s; took %v\", elapsed)\n+\n+\tobservedErr, _ := Commit to copy-at-build approach.

Steps:
1. Add Makefile target copy-skills in cli/Makefile that rsyncs skills/ddx/ into cli/internal/skills/ddx/ before every build. Example:
     copy-skills:
         rsync -a --delete ../skills/ddx/ ./internal/skills/ddx/
2. Wire copy-skills as a prerequisite of make build, make test, make dev (and any other targets that invoke go build or go test).
3. Update cli/internal/skills/embed.go to replace the current multi-dir embed line with: //go:embed all:ddx
4. Delete cli/internal/skills/ddx-{bead,agent,doctor,install,status,review,run}/ ŌĆö the new embed picks up the copied ddx/ tree instead. (Deletion of top-level /skills/ddx-* is Phase 2; this bead only touches cli/internal/skills/.)
5. Commit cli/internal/skills/ddx/ as a tracked copy so contributors who run go build directly (without make) still get a valid binary. Add a pre-commit hook (lefthook.yml) that re-runs make copy-skills if skills/ddx/ is dirty and cli/internal/skills/ddx/ is stale; this keeps the copy in sync without manual intervention.

In-scope files:
- cli/Makefile
- cli/internal/skills/embed.go
- cli/internal/skills/ddx/ (committed copy ŌĆö gets populated by copy-skills)
- lefthook.yml (add pre-commit hook)

Out-of-scope:
- skills/ddx/SKILL.md and reference files ŌĆö separate beads

     but docs/helix/00-discover/visuals/ contains only the six principle prompts ŌĆö
     no tool-graphic prompt files exist. There is no prompt input for V9 to consume
     even if generation infrastructure were available.

What is blocking
1. Missing image-generation skill (nano-banana-pro-openrouter) under
   .agents/skills/. Symlinks under .crush/skills/ and .pi/skills/ point at a
   directory that doesn't exist here.
2. No registered execution definition (`ddx exec list --json` is empty), so
   `ddx artifact regenerate` has no generator to dispatch to.
3. No registered tool-graphic artifacts in the document graph. Without sidecars
   declaring id + media_type + depends_on, regenerate can't resolve them.
4. No tool-graphic prompt sources in docs/helix/00-discover/visuals/, despite
   V8 (ddx-1848bbaa) being closed. V9's contract assumes those prompts exist.
5. The plan explicitly forbids manual prompt-file ŌåÆ image fallbacks
   (plan-2026-04-29-visual-suite.md:255). V9 must run through the artifact
   pipeline or not run at all.

What a follow-up attempt would need (in order)
1. Re-open or supersede V8 (ddx-1848bbaa) ŌĆö the four tool-graphic prompt files
   it was supposed to author are not in the worktree, so V9's input contract
   is also unmet. Verify the closure was substantive, not a no_changes.
2. Predecessor bead: install / vendor nano-banana-pro-openrouter into
   .agents/skills/ so the broken symlinks resolve.
3. Predecessor bead: author `ddx exec define` for the nano-banana-pro-openrouter
   generator with produces_artifact entries for each tool graphic plus
   tools-composite.
4. Predecessor bead: register the four tool-graphic artifacts and tools-composite
   in the document graph (sidecars with id, media_type image/png, depends_on
   the V8 prompts and DESIGN.md tokens).
5. Once 1ŌĆō4 hold, V9 collapses to: budget-tracked sequential
   `ddx artifact regenerate <each-tool-graphic>` then
   `ddx artifact regenerate tools-composite`, evaluating each render against
   the bead's acceptance criteria (placement, contrast, mobile crop, alt text,
   file size, metaphor parses without copy, brand-fit / no-kitsch),
   halting at $1.50/image or $10 total.

Recommendation
File the four predecessor beads above, block ddx-297e7b4b on them, and re-queue.
Do not escalate to a stronger model ŌĆö the constraint is missing infrastructure
and missing inputs, not model capability. This mirrors the V6 (ddx-ea3a7e23)
disposition exactly; resolving the infrastructure unblocks both the principles
and tools lenses simultaneously.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:435:{"acceptance":"- [ ] Schema added to .ddx/config.yaml for executions.mirror (kind, path, include, async)
- [ ] At least one mirror backend implemented (local-dir is the minimum; s3 preferred)
- [ ] execute-bead finalization triggers an async mirror upload that does not block bead completion
- [ ] mirror-index.jsonl appended with attempt_id, bead_id, mirror_uri, bytes, timestamp
- [ ] Mirror failures logged but do not fail the bead
- [ ] `ddx agent executions fetch <attempt_id>` retrieves a mirrored bundle back to local disk
- [ ] Path template supports {project}, {attempt_id}, {date}, {bead_id}
- [ ] Default config includes the full bundle (embedded included)
- [ ] Integration test: local-dir mirror roundtrip (run execute-bead stub, assert bundle mirrored, fetch it back, bytes match)
- [ ] Docs: update TD-010 (or add TD-011) with the mirror design and operator runbook","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T06:17:17Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"d810ddff35013426b0e3ec92f4e1ad1fcb45c777","created_at":"2026-04-15T18:59:41.811906799Z","description":"Execution bundles under .ddx/executions/<attempt>/ are the primary forensic record of what DDx automation did, but the most valuable part ŌĆö the per-iteration agent trace at embedded/agent-*.jsonl ŌĆö cannot reasonably be checked in. Individual traces can exceed 50 MB; a busy automation day produces hundreds of MB. Axon hit this on 2026-04-15 when GitHub warned about a 50.18 MB embedded trace file, and embedded/ is now gitignored per-repo.

We still want that level of detail for *every* run, for post-mortems, routing/model analysis, and training-data curation. We need a configurable out-of-band mirror.

## Proposed shape

1. **Archive target** configured via `.ddx/config.yaml` (and/or env var):
   - `executions.mirror.kind: local | s3 | gcs | http`
   - `executions.mirror.path: s3://bucket/ddx-executions/{project}/{attempt_id}/` (template with `{project}`, `{attempt_id}`, `{date}`, `{bead_id}`)
   - `executions.mirror.include: [manifest, prompt, result, usage, checks, embedded]` (allow excluding embedded for bandwidth-sensitive setups, but default = everything)
   - `executions.mirror.async: true` (upload in background after execute-bead returns so it never blocks the hot path)

2. **When to mirror**: at execute-bead finalization, after result.json is written. On failure, retry async with bounded attempts; log failures to `.ddx/agent-logs/mirror.log` but do not fail the bead.

3. **Index**: each mirrored bundle gets a pointer row appended to a local `.ddx/executions/mirror-index.jsonl` (attempt_id, bead_id, mirror_uri, uploaded_at, byte_size). This gives analysts a quick `jq` lookup without walking the remote store.

4. **Retrieval**: `ddx agent executions fetch <attempt_id>` pulls a bundle back to .ddx/executions/<attempt_id>/ for local inspection.

5. **GC**: optional local retention policy (`executions.retain_days`) that prunes old local bundles while the mirror keeps the full history.

## Non-goals

- Not a full audit trail service ŌĆö just a mirror of DDx's existing bundles.
- Not a replacement for git-tracked manifest/prompt/result/usage summaries ŌĆö those stay checked in.
- Not a distributed log aggregator ŌĆö one-shot copy per bundle, no streaming.

## Why now

Axon's ADR-018 session produced ~85 MB of execution artifacts in a single day; only 1.5 MB of that is useful as checked-in summary. Without a mirror, every project using DDx execute-bead faces the same tension: either gitignore the traces and lose the forensic data, or commit them and bloat the repo forever.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","resolved_model":"qwen3.6-35b-a3b","fallback_chain":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-17T18:23:42.944017231Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude model=qwen3.6-35b-a3b"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"execution_failed

Document-Driven Development eXperience

DDx is a toolkit for AI-assisted development that helps you:

ŌĆó Share templates, prompts, and patterns across projects
ŌĆó Maintain consistent development practices
ŌĆó Integrate AI tooling seamlessly
ŌĆó Contribute improvements back to the community

Get started:

  ddx list          See available resources
  ddx doctor        Check installation and diagnose issues

More information:

  Issues & Support: https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/issues

Usage:
  ddx [command]

Available Commands:
  agent       Invoke AI agents with harness dispatch, quorum, and session logging

  completion  Generate completion script
  config      Configure DDx settings
  doc         Document dependency graph and staleness tracking
  doctor      Check installation health and diagnose issues
  exec        Validate and run DDx execution definitions
  help        Help about any command
  init        Initialize DDx in current project

  jq          Process JSON with jq expressions
  list        List available DDx resources

After the skill, rules, and advisory CLI command exist, DDx needs user-facing documentation that explains Prose Quality Support without overclaiming. The docs must be clear that DDx provides quality guidance and deterministic checks, not AI detection.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- docs/prose-quality.md or the docs path chosen by TD-036
- README.md or docs index only for a concise link
- CHANGELOG.md if project convention requires an entry for user-facing CLI behavior

REQUIRED DOC CONTENT
- What Prose Quality Support does and does not do.
- How to run `ddx doc prose --changed`.
- Example finding output.
- Config examples for mode, vocabulary accept/reject, severity, includes/excludes, and advisory/blocking policy if supported.
- Guidance on when to use the human-writing-support skill.
- Explicit statement: not an AI detector and not detector bypass.

DDx is document-driven: PRDs, FEAT specs, technical designs, ADRs, bead descriptions, review prompts, and close notes are governing artifacts. When those documents drift into generic AI-like prose, vague importance language, or terminology inconsistency, agents receive weaker execution prompts and reviewers lose checkable design intent.

GOAL
Add DDx Prose Quality Support: DDx should help users and agents write clear, specific, voice-preserving project prose and flag common AI-writing tropes through advisory guidance plus deterministic checks. This is not an AI detector and must not be framed as detector bypass.

PROPOSED SLICES
1. Define the FEAT product contract for prose quality support.
2. Define the technical design for a deterministic prose quality pipeline.
3. Add fixtures/golden expected findings to keep false positives under control.
4. Ship a default-plugin human-writing-support skill.
5. Add default rules and DDx vocabulary.
6. Add ddx doc prose --changed as the first advisory CLI surface.
7. Document configuration and non-goals.
8. Later, integrate prose findings into bead review after noise is known.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement AI-authorship detection. Do not make prose findings blocking by default. Do not wire this into ddx work or bead review until the advisory doc command has landed and has fixture coverage.","id":"ddx-ccda7a32","issue_type":"epic","labels":["phase:2","area:docs","area:skills","area:cli","kind:platform","prose-quality"],"priority":2,"status":"open","title":"EPIC: Add DDx Prose Quality Support","updated_at":"2026-05-05T18:10:44.395302673Z"}

Failing citations block the PR.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T06:37:50Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"790f3b9752c1b3b289fd8f3725291fd5b16ed067","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:06:41.260303435Z","description":"## Problem

No single document traces the provider/model/harness resolution path from CLI flags to the final HTTP request. SD-015 and SD-023 are SPECS ŌĆö they describe what should happen in 5 routing modes. The code lives across routing.go, discovery.go, agent_runner.go, runner.go, and agent_cmd.go. A newcomer debugging a routing bug must infer the flow by reading all of them.

The audit for epic ddx-2d974641 turned up a RouteRequest struct missing its Provider field, and a parallel RunOptions.Provider path that bypasses routing ŌĆö neither of which is documented. This is the kind of hidden structural defect a Resolution Path document would have flagged on first read.

## Fix

Write docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md (or similar location; coordinate with SD-015's author). The document traces one concrete invocation end-to-end:

    ddx work --once --local --provider vidar-omlx --model-ref qwen/qwen3.6

Walk through every layer, with file:line citations:

1. **CLI parse** ŌĆö cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go flag extraction (line references).

2. **RouteFlags -> RouteRequest normalization** ŌĆö routing.go:NormalizeRouteRequest. What each CLI flag maps to; which flags are discarded when they don't match an expected shape; the current gap around --provider.

3. **Discovery probe** ŌĆö discovery.go:DiscoverProviderModels. When it fires; what it caches; the 30s TTL; how failures are handled.

4. **Candidate planning** ŌĆö routing.go:BuildCandidatePlans / evaluateCandidate. For each registered harness, the state map lookup and the viability checks.

5. **Fuzzy matching** ŌĆö discovery.go:FuzzyMatchModel. Pool selection (global vs per-provider), normalization (current gaps), prefix + tiebreak algorithm.

6. **Scoring** ŌĆö routing.go:scoreCandidate. Per-profile scoring, cost class, historical success rate, provider-affinity bonus (when implemented).

7. **Candidate ranking** ŌĆö routing.go:RankCandidates / SelectBestCandidate. Non-viable rejection, stable tiebreak.

8. **Dispatch** ŌĆö runner.go:Run. Harness-specific branches (virtual, agent, script, HTTP-provider-embedded, default exec). For embedded: agent_runner.go:RunAgent -> resolveEmbeddedAgentProvider -> agent library call.

9. **Wire-level request** ŌĆö ddx-agent builds the HTTP body (system_prompt + messages + tools), sends to the provider. What the server receives.

10. **Response parsing** ŌĆö streaming or non-streaming decode; where 'unexpected end of JSON input' (ddx-6a5dfe35) surfaces.

Include a SEQUENCE DIAGRAM (mermaid or ASCII) showing the layers.

Include a GAP TABLE at the top listing every currently-missing piece (as of whatever the SD-015 implementation state is) with links to the specific beads tracking each gap.

Keep the document MAINTAINED alongside the code: update it when RouteRequest gains a field, when a new routing mode lands, when fuzzy match gains a normalization step.

## Files likely touched

- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md (new)
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md ŌĆö add a single-line pointer at the top of the 'Overview' section
- skills/ddx/reference/agents.md ŌĆö add a one-line pointer in 'Harness / profile / persona dispatch' section so agents consulting the skill can follow up to the full trace when needed

## Out of scope

- Rewriting SD-015 or SD-023 ŌĆö this doc complements them; they describe intent, this describes current reality.
- Documenting future routing modes that haven't {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01GZzJDTKtx6XSP7gKPQq28S","type":"tool_result","content":"282\t//     harness binary receives the model string as-is and can error clearly.\n283\t//     - Otherwise: try catalog resolution:\n284\t//     - If known in catalog: ModelRef.\n285\t//     - If not known: ModelPin (exact pin, bypasses catalog).\n286\t//  3. If flags.Profile is set ŌåÆ Profile.\n287\t//  4. Else fall back to cfg.Model (same catalog resolution) or cfg.Profile.\n288\t//  5. If nothing is set ŌåÆ cfg.Harness becomes HarnessOverride (legacy fallback).\n289\tfunc NormalizeRouteRequest(flags RouteFlags, cfg Config, catalog *Catalog) RouteRequest {\n290\t\tif catalog == nil {\n291\t\t\tcatalog = BuiltinCatalog\n292\t\t}\n293\t\n294\t\treq := RouteRequest{\n295\t\t\tEffort:      flags.Effort,\n296\t\t\tPermissions: flags.Permissions,\n297\t\t}\n298\t\n299\t\t// 1. Harness override constrains routing to one harness.\n300\t\t//    --provider that names a known harness (or alias like \"local\") also\n301\t\t//    pins the harness so the routing engine never falls through to a\n302\t\t//    different harness.\n303\t\tif flags.Harness != \"\" {\n304\t\t\treq.HarnessOverride = flags.Harness\n305\t\t} else if flags.Provider != \"\" {\n306\t\t\tresolved := resolveHarnessAlias(flags.Provider)\n307\t\t\t// Only treat as harness override if it names a registered harness;\n308\t\t\t// otherwise it's a ddx-agent provider name (e.g. \"vidar\") and\n309\t\t\t// should not constrain harness selection.\n310\t\t\tif _, ok := builtinHarnesses[resolved]; ok {\n311\t\t\t\treq.HarnessOverride = resolved"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01BpB5ybRXLsC8V3pvMEmJe9","session_id":"d932d409-7007-4d29-af52-a78f704e35c0","uuid":"3708917d-10f5-4047-87a8-eca15bf792b1","timestamp":"2026-04-18T06:48:52.477Z"}

NO CHANGES committed in this attempt.

## Why this bead is currently blocked

The bead description has four parts. Three of the four are blocked on a
prerequisite that has not landed:

(a) "Delete DDx reimplementations of upstream subcommands"
    (agent_list/capabilities/doctor/check/models/providers/route_status/
    catalog/reindex/benchmark/replay)

(c) "Register DDx overrides on mounted subtree (deprecations + condense)"

Both presuppose a mounted upstream Cobra root grafted under `ddx agent`
ŌĆö i.e. `cli(ddx).agent(load_cli(agent))` per FEAT-001-cli.md:69 and
plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md:28.

The CONTRACT-003 audit (committed 08a40b69) explicitly identifies this
as a gap (docs/helix/02-design/contract-003-audit-2026-04-29.md:23ŌĆō89):

    > DDx cannot write `agentcli.NewRootCommand()` against the current
    > contract.
    >
    > The plan's "delete with prejudice" list (agent_list,
    > agent_capabilities, agent_doctor, agent_check, agent_models,
    > agent_providers, agent_route_status, agent_catalog,
    > agent_reindex) presupposes that upstream owns these subcommands
    > inside a mountable tree. Today they exist only as flag-driven
    > branches in cmd/agent/main.go that DDx cannot consume
    > programmatically.
    >
    > Plan bead #6 ("open CONTRACT-003 amendment in `~/Projects/agent`
    > if #5 surfaces gaps") is **required, not conditional**, because
    > of this gap.

Concretely:

  * cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go (lines 123ŌĆō141) wires every soon-to-be-deleted
    file via `cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentCheckCommand())`,
    `newAgentModelsCommand()`, `newAgentProvidersCommand()`,
    `newAgentRouteStatusCommand()`, `newAgentListCommand()`,
    `newAgentCapabilitiesCommand()`, `newAgentDoctorCommand()`,
    `newAgentBenchmarkCommand()`, `newAgentCatalogCommand()`,
    `newAgentReplayCommand()`. Deleting any of those `agent_*.go`
    files without the upstream `MountCLI` replacement would leave
    agent_cmd.go referencing missing constructors and break the build.
  * The replacement (`cmd.AddCommand(agent.MountCLI(...))`) requires
    the upstream module to expose `MountCLI` from a non-`internal/`
    package, which the audit specifies as the upstream amendment
    CONTRACT-003-A (port `cmd/agent/main.go` from `flag` to `cobra`,
    move into `agent/cli`, re-export `MountCLI`, bump module version).
    That work has not happened in `~/Projects/agent`.

(b) "Migrate DDx-only subcommands"
    (agent_executions ŌåÆ tries_*; agent_log ŌåÆ runs_log;
     agent_metrics ŌåÆ runs_metrics; agent_usage folded into runs_metrics;
     agent_workers ŌåÆ work_workers)

This portion is mechanically possible inside DDx alone, but per the
plan (line 208) beads #3, #7, #8, #15 must merge atomically ŌĆö with #15
landing in isolation, the new namespaces (`ddx runs`, `ddx tries`,
`ddx work workers`) need top-level registration that has no analogue
yet in command_factory_commands.go. The implementation P2 phase of the
plan (lines 196ŌĆō204) lists "CLI: `ddx runs` / `ddx tries` / `ddx work
workers` namespaces (subsuming migrated subcommands)" as separate work
that comes after the frame/design beads. Doing only (b) here, without
(a) and (c), produces a confusing CLI surface where the same evidence
is reachable both as `ddx agent executions` and `ddx tries list`, and
it strands the deprecation messages that need to point at the new
verbs.

(d) "Update scripts/hooks/library skills/personas/docs/README/demos to
    new verbs"

This depends on the new verbs (`ddx run`, `ddx try`, `ddx runs *`,
`ddx tries *`, `ddx work workers *`) actually existing as runnable
commands so that the updated scripts/skills run green. `ddx run` and
`ddx try` are not registered today (grep for `Use:.*\"run\"` /
`Use:.*\"try\"` against cli/cmd/*.go shows only the `ddx agent run`
inner command). Updating scripts/eval-skill.sh,
scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh, scripts/test-doc-snippets.sh,
scripts/demos/06-full-journey.sh,
library/.agents/skills/ddx-refine-plan/SKILL.md, etc. to call
non-existent verbs would break those flows immediately and would
specifically break the in-flight `ddx-refine-plan` skill that produced
this very plan (plan line 208).

## What an unblocked attempt needs

Roughly in dependency order:

1. Upstream amendment CONTRACT-003-A merged in `~/Projects/agent`:
   port to cobra, move commands to `agent/cli`, export `MountCLI`,
   tag a release, and bump `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent` in
   DDx's go.mod (no replace directive ŌĆö see memory
   feedback_no_local_replace).
2. Top-level `ddx run` and `ddx try` registered as first-class
   commands under CommandFactory (the implementation P2 thread of the
   plan).
3. `ddx runs`, `ddx tries`, `ddx work workers` namespace skeletons
   registered, even if their subcommand implementations are then
   ported in this bead.

Once those are in, the present bead becomes:

  * Replace the 11+ enumerated `cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgent*Command())`
    calls in agent_cmd.go with `cmd.AddCommand(agent.MountCLI(...))`,
    plus deprecation override registrations for `agent run`,
    `agent execute-bead`, `agent execute-loop`, and the override for
    `agent condense` (DDx-specific filter).
  * `git rm` agent_check.go, agent_models.go, agent_providers.go,
    agent_route_status.go (the only "delete with prejudice" files
    that actually exist in DDx today; agent_list/capabilities/doctor/
    catalog/reindex/benchmark/replay only exist as inline functions
    inside agent_cmd.go and would be removed by the agent_cmd.go
    rewrite).
  * Move agent_executions.go ŌåÆ tries_*.go; agent_metrics.go ŌåÆ
    runs_metrics.go (folding agent_usage.go into it);
    agent_workers.go ŌåÆ work_workers.go; rename `newAgentLogCommand`
    in agent_cmd.go into `runs_log.go`. Update tests in lockstep.
  * Sweep scripts/hooks/library skills/personas/docs/README/demos to
    swap old verbs for new (e.g. `ddx agent run` ŌåÆ `ddx run`,
    `ddx agent execute-loop` ŌåÆ `ddx work`,
    `ddx agent execute-bead` ŌåÆ `ddx try`,
    `ddx agent log/metrics/usage/executions/workers` ŌåÆ the new
    `runs/tries/work workers` paths). Specific known references:
    scripts/eval-skill.sh, scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh,
    scripts/test-doc-snippets.sh, scripts/demos/06-full-journey.sh,
    library/.agents/skills/ddx-refine-plan/SKILL.md,
    library/personas/README.md, cli/cmd/init.go:757,
    cli/cmd/bead_review.go:30ŌĆō33, plus a wider grep across
    docs/**/*.md and library/**/*.md.

## What is done in this attempt

Nothing on disk ŌĆö but the verification above (audit cross-reference,
agent_cmd.go wiring inspection, FEAT-001 surface inspection,
search for top-level `run`/`try` registrations) is captured here so
the next attempt can move directly into execution once bead #6 lands.

## Recommended next steps

- Park this bead behind bead #6 (upstream CONTRACT-003-A amendment)
  and the P2 implementation beads that introduce top-level
  `ddx run` / `ddx try` and the `runs`/`tries`/`work workers`
  namespace skeletons.
- Re-attempt with the same id once those prerequisites have merged.
  At that point this bead is mechanical: a contained refactor of
  agent_cmd.go plus a directed grep-and-edit sweep of the call sites
  enumerated above.

DDx should guide agents while they write or edit prose, separate from deterministic post-write checks. This bead ships a default-plugin skill named human-writing-support that adapts the stronger advisory guidance from human-writing-style approaches to DDx: preserve voice, prefer specific detail, avoid generic AI-like tropes, and do not trade away technical precision.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- library/.agents/skills/ddx/human-writing-support/SKILL.md or the default-plugin skill path established by FEAT-011/TD-036
- skills/ddx/human-writing-support/SKILL.md and .agents/.claude materialized copies only if this repo maintains generated checked-in mirrors
- skill validation/eval fixtures under the existing skills validation surface

REQUIRED SKILL BEHAVIOR
- Trigger for writing, rewriting, editing, or reviewing DDx prose, specs, ADRs, beads, docs, website copy, and public prose.
- Explicitly says it is not an AI detector or detector-bypass tool.
- Preserves user voice and technical precision.
- Applies different expectations for technical, planning, and public prose.
- Encourages concrete/checkable detail over generic importance language.
- Directs agents to run deterministic prose checks when available.

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- CLI checker implementation.
- Rule pack implementation.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:1180:{"acceptance":"All child beads closed. skills/ddx/ exists with SKILL.md + reference/{beads,work,review,agents,status}.md + evals/routing.jsonl. ddx init in a scratch dir produces exactly one skill directory (.claude/skills/ddx/). make eval-skill passes on --harness claude and --harness codex. cd cli && go test ./... passes.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:03:39.056667381Z","description":"Replace the 7 sibling ddx-* skills with a single `ddx` skill using progressive disclosure (SKILL.md + reference/ subfiles) per the agentskills.io open standard. Portable across Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, and other skills-compatible harnesses.

Plan file: /home/erik/.claude/plans/let-s-review-the-skills-synchronous-kite.md (reference only ŌĆö do not require reading it; beads inline the context).

Governing spec: docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md (rewritten to reflect this design).

Motivation: Today ddx init ships 2 of 7 embedded skills, skill trees have drifted between /skills/ and cli/internal/skills/, HELIX opinions leak into ddx-bead, and users must guess between /ddx-run vs /ddx-work vs /ddx-bead. The single-skill design routes every natural-language intent ("do work", "drain the queue", "review this") into the same skill, then into the right reference file.

.ddx/executions/20260430T004352-d9a11b36/result.json:9:  "no_changes_rationale": "Bead ddx-ebf3c09b ŌĆö repo-wide verb migration + agent_*.go cleanup
===================================================================

NO CHANGES committed in this attempt.

## Why this bead is currently blocked

The bead description has four parts. Three of the four are blocked on a
prerequisite that has not landed:

(a) "Delete DDx reimplementations of upstream subcommands"
    (agent_list/capabilities/doctor/check/models/providers/route_status/
    catalog/reindex/benchmark/replay)

(c) "Register DDx overrides on mounted subtree (deprecations + condense)"

Both presuppose a mounted upstream Cobra root grafted under `ddx agent`
ŌĆö i.e. `cli(ddx).agent(load_cli(agent))` per FEAT-001-cli.md:69 and
plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md:28.

The CONTRACT-003 audit (committed 08a40b69) explicitly identifies this
as a gap (docs/helix/02-design/contract-003-audit-2026-04-29.md:23ŌĆō89):

    > DDx cannot write `agentcli.NewRootCommand()` against the current
    > contract.
    >
    > The plan's "delete with prejudice" list (agent_list,
    > agent_capabilities, agent_doctor, agent_check, agent_models,
    > agent_providers, agent_route_status, agent_catalog,
    > agent_reindex) presupposes that upstream owns these subcommands
    > inside a mountable tree. Today they exist only as flag-driven
    > branches in cmd/agent/main.go that DDx cannot consume
    > programmatically.
    >
    > Plan bead #6 ("open CONTRACT-003 amendment in `~/Projects/agent`
    > if #5 surfaces gaps") is **required, not conditional**, because
    > of this gap.

Concretely:

  * cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go (lines 123ŌĆō141) wires every soon-to-be-deleted
    file via `cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentCheckCommand())`,
    `newAgentModelsCommand()`, `newAgentProvidersCommand()`,
    `newAgentRouteStatusCommand()`, `newAgentListCommand()`,
    `newAgentCapabilitiesCommand()`, `newAgentDoctorCommand()`,
    `newAgentBenchmarkCommand()`, `newAgentCatalogCommand()`,
    `newAgentReplayCommand()`. Deleting any of those `agent_*.go`
    files without the upstream `MountCLI` replacement would leave
    agent_cmd.go referencing missing constructors and break the build.
  * The replacement (`cmd.AddCommand(agent.MountCLI(...))`) requires
    the upstream module to expose `MountCLI` from a non-`internal/`
    package, which the audit specifies as the upstream amendment
    CONTRACT-003-A (port `cmd/agent/main.go` from `flag` to `cobra`,
    move into `agent/cli`, re-export `MountCLI`, bump module version).
    That work has not happened in `~/Projects/agent`.

(b) "Migrate DDx-only subcommands"
    (agent_executions ŌåÆ tries_*; agent_log ŌåÆ runs_log;
     agent_metrics ŌåÆ runs_metrics; agent_usage folded into runs_metrics;
     agent_workers ŌåÆ work_workers)

This portion is mechanically possible inside DDx alone, but per the
plan (line 208) beads #3, #7, #8, #15 must merge atomically ŌĆö with #15
landing in isolation, the new namespaces (`ddx runs`, `ddx tries`,
`ddx work workers`) need top-level registration that has no analogue
yet in command_factory_commands.go. The implementation P2 phase of the
plan (lines 196ŌĆō204) lists "CLI: `ddx runs` / `ddx tries` / `ddx work
workers` namespaces (subsuming migrated subcommands)" as separate work
that comes after the frame/design beads. Doing only (b) here, without
(a) and (c), produces a confusing CLI surface where the same evidence
is reachable both as `ddx agent executions` and `ddx tries list`, and
it strands the deprecation messages that need to point at the new
verbs.

(d) "Update scripts/hooks/library skills/personas/docs/README/demos to
    new verbs"

This depends on the new verbs (`ddx run`, `ddx try`, `ddx runs *`,
`ddx tries *`, `ddx work workers *`) actually existing as runnable
commands so that the updated scripts/skills run green. `ddx run` and
`ddx try` are not registered today (grep for `Use:.*\"run\"` /
`Use:.*\"try\"` against cli/cmd/*.go shows only the `ddx agent run`
inner command). Updating scripts/eval-skill.sh,
scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh, scripts/test-doc-snippets.sh,
scripts/demos/06-full-journey.sh,
library/.agents/skills/ddx-refine-plan/SKILL.md, etc. to call
non-existent verbs would break those flows immediately and would
specifically break the in-flight `ddx-refine-plan` skill that produced
this very plan (plan line 208).

## What an unblocked attempt needs

Roughly in dependency order:

1. Upstream amendment CONTRACT-003-A merged in `~/Projects/agent`:
   port to cobra, move commands to `agent/cli`, export `MountCLI`,
   tag a release, and bump `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent` in
   DDx's go.mod (no replace directive ŌĆö see memory
   feedback_no_local_replace).
2. Top-level `ddx run` and `ddx try` registered as first-class
   commands under CommandFactory (the implementation P2 thread of the
   plan).
3. `ddx runs`, `ddx tries`, `ddx work workers` namespace skeletons
   registered, even if their subcommand implementations are then
   ported in this bead.

Once those are in, the present bead becomes:

  * Replace the 11+ enumerated `cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgent*Command())`
    calls in agent_cmd.go with `cmd.AddCommand(agent.MountCLI(...))`,
    plus deprecation override registrations for `agent run`,

name: ddx
version: 0.4.7
description: DDx default library ŌĆö prompts, personas, MCP server configs, environment templates, and skills
type: plugin
source: https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx
api_version: "1"
keywords:
  - library
  - prompts
  - personas
  - mcp
  - default
  - skills
install:
  root:
    source: library
    target: .ddx/plugins/ddx
  skills:
    - source: .agents/skills/
      target: .agents/skills/
    - source: .agents/skills/
      target: .claude/skills/
    - source: .agents/skills/
      target: ~/.agents/skills/
    - source: .agents/skills/
      target: ~/.claude/skills/

IŌĆÖm reading the `copy-skills` target now so I can tell whether `library/.agents/...` is the source or whether I need to patch the generated mirrors directly.

data.output:
echo "frontend-verify: $$missing referenced asset(s) missing ŌĆö run 'make frontend-rebuild'"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi; \
	echo "  ok"

# Copy the canonical top-level skills/ddx/ tree into cli/internal/skills/ddx/
# so //go:embed (which cannot traverse upward out of the package) picks it up.
# Runs as a prereq of build/test/dev so contributors never see stale embedded
# skills. rsync --delete keeps the destination in exact sync.
SKILLS_SRC := ../skills/ddx
SKILLS_DST := internal/skills/ddx
copy-skills:
	@if [ ! -d "$(SKILLS_SRC)" ]; then \
		echo "error: $(SKILLS_SRC) not found (run from cli/)"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@rsync -a --delete $(SKILLS_SRC)/ $(SKILLS_DST)/

# Build for current platform
build: copy-skills frontend-build
	@echo "Building DDx CLI..."
	@mkdir -p $(BUILD_DIR)
	go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx .

# Build for all platforms
build-all: clean deps copy-skills
	@echo "Building for all platforms..."
	@mkdir -p $(BUILD_DIR)
	@echo "Building current platform..."
	go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx .
	@for platform in $(PLATFORMS); do \
		OS=$$(echo $$platform | cut -d'/' -f1); \
		ARCH=$$(echo $$platform | cut -d'/' -f2); \
		echo "Building $$OS/$$ARCH..."; \
		GOOS=$$OS GOARCH=$$ARCH go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx-$$OS-$$ARCH .; \
		if [ $$OS = "windows" ]; then \
			mv $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx-$$OS-$$ARCH $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx-$$OS-$$ARCH.exe; \
		fi; \
	done

# Install dependencies
deps:

.agents/skills/library/.agents/skills/human-writing-support
.agents/skills/library/.agents/skills/human-writing-support/SKILL.md

.claude/skills/library/.agents/skills/human-writing-support
.claude/skills/library/.agents/skills/human-writing-support/SKILL.md

Desc:     CONTEXT
DDx should guide agents while they write or edit prose, separate from deterministic post-write checks. This bead ships a default-plugin skill named human-writing-support that adapts the stronger advisory guidance from human-writing-style approaches to DDx: preserve voice, prefer specific detail, avoid generic AI-like tropes, and do not trade away technical precision.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- library/.agents/skills/ddx/human-writing-support/SKILL.md or the default-plugin skill path established by FEAT-011/TD-036
- skills/ddx/human-writing-support/SKILL.md and .agents/.claude materialized copies only if this repo maintains generated checked-in mirrors
- skill validation/eval fixtures under the existing skills validation surface

REQUIRED SKILL BEHAVIOR
- Trigger for writing, rewriting, editing, or reviewing DDx prose, specs, ADRs, beads, docs, website copy, and public prose.
- Explicitly says it is not an AI detector or detector-bypass tool.
